title: Passing JSON object results in [object Object]
symptom: When attempting to pass a JSON object to an execute command node, the output displays as '[object Object]'. This indicates that the object is not being properly serialized into a string format.
not_this_if: The parameter is a simple string without spaces
root_cause: The execute command node requires a string input, but when a JSON object is passed directly, it is converted to a string representation that results in '[object Object]'. This happens because JavaScript's default behavior for object-to-string conversion does not serialize the object's contents.
fix_steps: Use JSON.stringify() to convert the JSON object to a string.
Apply encodeURI() to the string to handle spaces and special characters.
Pass the encoded string to the execute command node.
agent_instruction: Transform the JSON object to a string using JSON.stringify() and encode it with encodeURI() before passing it to the execute command node.
